data.name: Intro_Text
data.value: A quick snapshot comparing the SNG's own-source revenues with similar domestic or international peers. Even if the snapshot looks strong (or suggests limited room), the bottom-up analysis remains the main reference because it measures gaps directly by revenue stream. Where benchmark data is not available, skip this step and go to bottom-up.

data.name: GovUnit1Helper
data.value: e.g. region, state, province, county, department, governorate

data.name: Level1Final
data.value: This assessment applies to the selected Level 1 unit as a whole

data.name: GovUnit2Helper
data.value: e.g. city, municipality, district, canton, commune

data.name: Level2Final
data.value: This assessment applies to the selected Level 2 unit as a whole

data.name: CurrencyOverride
data.value: Use a different currency for analysis outputs

data.name: CurrencyOverrideHelp
data.value: This changes the display currency for all analysis outputs.

data.name: FrontierSubtitle
data.value: Compare against domestic peer subnational governments

data.name: SelectCountryForFrontier
data.value: Select a country and subnational government to view OSR frontier analysis

data.name: SelectSngForAnalysis
data.value: Select a subnational government to view the OSR frontier analysis

data.name: Metric_FrontierMultiplier
data.value: Peer frontier multiplier (Top-20% avg OSR/GDP per capita)

data.name: Chart_BarTitle
data.value: Current OSR vs Frontier Benchmark per capita

data.name: Result_Placeholder
data.value: Select a subnational government to view the analysis result.

data.name: CrossCountry_Subtitle
data.value: The following analyses compare your country's position in the global fiscal decentralisation space.

data.name: CrossCountry_ResultPlaceholder
data.value: Select a country to view the analysis result.

data.name: JS_Result_LargeGap
data.value: Current OSR per capita is about {0}% of the peer frontier benchmark within the country (around {1} compared with a benchmark of {2}, leaving an indicative gap of {3}). This points to a large gap relative to today's top domestic performers under the same rules. The frontier is only a conservative peer benchmark; the ROSRA bottom-up analysis is needed to see how much of this gap can realistically be closed through administrative improvements in each OSR stream.

data.name: JS_Result_MeaningfulGap
data.value: Current OSR per capita is about {0}% of the peer frontier benchmark within the country (around {1} compared with a benchmark of {2}, leaving an indicative gap of {3}). This indicates a meaningful but not extreme gap compared with the better-performing SNGs under current rules. The frontier gives a quick sense of peer-based potential; the ROSRA bottom-up analysis will show, stream by stream, how much of this gap could be closed through improved administration.

data.name: JS_Result_CloseToFrontier
data.value: Current OSR per capita is about {0}% of the peer frontier benchmark within the country (around {1} compared with a benchmark of {2}, leaving an indicative gap of {3}). This suggests that, under current national rules, the city is relatively close to observed domestic good practice. However, the frontier itself may still be below the true long-term potential; ROSRA's bottom-up analysis remains essential to uncover remaining administrative gaps by stream and any further realistic revenue gains.

data.name: JS_CC_BothHigh
data.value: Funding is close to the peer frontier ({0}%) and autonomy is also relatively high ({1}%). This points to a comparatively large and discretionary SNG envelope; further improvements will mainly need to be identified through the ROSRA administrative gap analysis.

data.name: JS_CC_FundingHighAutLow
data.value: Funding is close to the peer frontier ({0}%), while autonomy is noticeably lower ({1}%). This suggests that the main constraint may lie in how revenues are shared and conditioned, even though there is still scope for administrative strengthening at the local level.

data.name: JS_CC_FundingLowAutHigh
data.value: Funding is below the peer frontier ({0}%) but autonomy is relatively strong ({1}%). This combination suggests that the overall SNG envelope is tight, even if local discretion over revenues is not unusually low. Administrative improvements remain relevant, and the adequacy of the funding assignment can also be discussed where needed.

data.name: JS_CC_BothLow
data.value: Both funding ({0}% of the frontier) and autonomy ({1}%) are below peer benchmarks. This pattern indicates that SNGs may face constraints in both the size and the structure of their revenue; administrative options should be explored first, alongside a careful discussion of possible intergov. or policy adjust.
